Article Title: When disappearing wetlands left birds with nowhere safe to nest, the United States began building artificial islands that now shelter thousands of breeding waterbirds
Article Text:
A wide aerial shot showing hundreds or thousands of birds nesting on an artificial island, surrounded by water. Image Credit: Gemini

Global loss of wetlands

Building islands predators cannot reach

A Black Skimmer skimming for food. Image Credit: Wikimedia Commons

Artificial islands are now being used to help wetlands in the United States

Helping hands instead of a substitute for nature

For thousands of years, wetlands have provided natural nesting, feeding, and resting habitat for waterbirds. However, owing to changes in urbanization, agriculture, and water systems around the world, many species of birds have lost their homes. As a result, conservationists in countries such as the U.S. have begun building artificial islands in wetlands to give birds safer places to breed.Although artificial habitats cannot replace natural wetlands, studies suggest they can be important conservation tools.Wetlands are some of the most productive ecosystems in terms of biology. They serve a number of purposes in supporting wildlife, maintaining water quality, storing carbon, and reducing the effects of flooding. Human intervention has led to the loss of many wetlands across the globe. In an article published in PLOS ONE in 2018, the decline in wetlands has affected the ecological functions of wetlands and has endangered the biodiversity, especially waterbirds, which depend on wetlands for food and reproduction.The authors, Efthymia Giosa, Christos Mammides, and Savvas Zotos, surveyed 19 wetlands on Cyprus, 13 artificial and 6 natural, using monthly bird counts from 2009 to 2011, and found that larger, shallower wetlands held more species. They also reported that bird diversity was higher where wetlands were surrounded by more hunting reserves and fewer roads, suggesting that even artificial wetlands can support important waterbird communities when disturbance is limited.Their findings indicated that natural wetlands were home to a greater number of species as well as a higher abundance of birds. Some man-made wetlands could support equally diverse bird populations, provided they had favorable qualities like large size and shallow water.Finding water is one challenge for waterbirds, but finding safe nesting sites is often harder. Artificial nesting islands help by creating distance between breeding waterbirds and land predators such as foxes, raccoons and other mammals that can destroy eggs and chicks.Studies conducted in the United States have investigated how island design affects bird breeding outcomes. In an article written in the Journal of Wildlife Management , researchers studied waterbird nesting habitat islands in wetlands and noted that nest numbers were affected by certain island features, including distance from shore. It was observed that those islands not near the shores had better breeding success for nesting birds due to reduced predator access.In North America, artificial islands have been used to help species that live in wetland ecosystems.In the prairie region of the United States, scientists have found that artificial islands in wetlands can provide crucial nesting grounds for waterfowl. The research found that mallards and Canada geese used the islands for safety. Similar strategies have also been used in coastal wetlands. For example, research has examined how managed wetland islands can create breeding grounds for birds such as American avocets, black-necked stilts and Forster’s terns in California’s San Francisco Bay Area.In a study conducted by the US Geological Survey , it was found that the design of the island itself was significant in influencing the waterbird nesting success and abundance. It was stressed that strategically designed islands could be important breeding habitats.Natural wetlands are complex ecosystems that have developed over thousands of years and include plant communities, insects, fish, and microscopic organisms that man-made wetlands cannot fully mimic. The Cyprus research revealed that although some man-made wetlands had high bird diversity, natural wetlands were more valuable due to a higher presence of birds and specialists.Experts suggested that man-made wetlands should be seen as a supplement to wildlife conservation, not a replacement for natural wetlands. As development and climate change threaten wetlands, conservationists have turned to artificial nesting islands.For birds that have lost their natural habitats, the artificial islands provide an important breeding ground.
Article Summary: Wetlands are some of the most productive ecosystems in terms of biology. They serve a number of purposes in supporting wildlife, maintaining water quality, storing carbon, and reducing the effects of flooding. Human intervention has led to the loss of many wetlands across the globe. As development and climate change threaten wetlands, conservationists have turned to artificial nesting islands.

Article Title: California's collapsing cliffs do warn before they give way; they begin moving hours before disaster strikes, scientists found
Article Text:
California's iconic cliffs face erosion, prompting new safety measures. Advanced sensors now detect ground deformation indicating potential collapses. Scientists successfully warned authorities about a Del Mar landslide days prior. Image credit: Adam Young/ Coastal Processes Group at Scripps Institution of Oceanography

California Coastal Landslide Early Warning Research

Heartbeat of the bluffs

This technology allows for early warnings to coastal communities and beachgoers. Future applications may include smartphone apps for enhanced public safety planning. Image Credit: Jesse Kerr/ Coastal Processes Group at Scripps Institution of Oceanography

Designing a safer coastal lifestyle

The picturesque coastlines of California have long been iconic worldwide. For decades now, the spectacular golden cliffs with the pounding waves of the Pacific Ocean in the background have acted as the perfect setting for beach road trips, surfing in the early mornings, and relaxing beach strolls. The cliffs are a defining part of California's coastal landscape and attract many visitors. However, erosion and landslides have prompted coastal towns to rethink their relationship with the coast.According to data from the Scripps Institution of Oceanography, roughly seventy per cent of the California coast is made up of eroding coastal cliffs. As sea levels rise and weather patterns become more variable, these cliffs face pressure from waves below and rainwater above. For decades, beachgoers and city planners alike viewed cliff collapses as sudden, unpredictable catastrophes. However, new technology is showing that these earth movements can be detected before a collapse.To grasp the change in this landscape, you have to focus on a study by the Scripps Institution of Oceanography, called the report . Led by scientists specialising in coastal geomorphology and geophysics, the study involved placing sensors in the ground near some of the most vulnerable beaches.The report found that coastal bluffs send out signals well before a collapse.The key to finding those elusive indicators lies in using highly sensitive geophysical instruments that were initially developed to measure the slight movements of earthquake fault zones. To detect when a cliff begins to collapse, scientists used high-accuracy tiltmeters placed underground. Those highly sophisticated instruments can measure ground tilting to an accuracy of 10 micrometres over a one-metre baseline, which is roughly equivalent to one-eighth the width of a human hair. In addition to these tiltmeters, the research team installed specialised fibre optic cables known as extensometers perpendicular to the cliff edge. These fibre optic sensors extend or compress if any ground deformation occurs, allowing researchers to detect movements as tiny as a nanometer, or one-billionth of a meter.According to the California Coastal Landslide Early Warning Research report, this sophisticated array of sensors allowed scientists to gather real-time data across several active test sites, including the railway corridor in Del Mar and San Elijo State Beach. The true value of this technology became undeniable on 21 April 2024, when an estimated two hundred tons of material suddenly broke away from a cliffside in Del Mar. Days before the landslide, the tiltmeter network picked up a steady, accelerating pattern of ground deformation during intermittent rainfall soaking into the coastal soil. Because the instruments sent data in real time indicating that a collapse was highly likely, Scripps researchers were able to notify coastal managers two days before the failure occurred.The finer nuances of how these cliffs move due to the pressures on them are further revealed by a study conducted in the Journal of Geophysical Research: Earth Surface. Written by researchers themselves, the paper reveals how five separate cliff failures were observed during the long period of monitoring. In all cases, the in-ground sensors detected a tipping point in time, suggesting that cliffs accelerate before collapse.As prediction techniques improve, researchers are considering everyday applications. Since the research suggests some collapses show warning signs, local authorities may be able to develop warning systems for citizens. Like a weather app, a future warning system could alert beachgoers when a section of cliff is under increased stress.The research team also developed an experimental smartphone app to forecast beach width several days in advance. Because a wider sandy beach can increase distance from unstable cliffs, tracking wave heights and tide cycles may help inform safety planning. By integrating real-time ground monitoring with public safety applications, coastal cities may reduce some of the risks associated with living along the coast.
Article Summary: Advanced sensors now detect ground deformation indicating potential collapses. Scientists successfully warned authorities about a Del Mar landslide days prior. As sea levels rise and weather patterns become more variable, these cliffs face pressure from waves below and rainwater above. Future applications may include smartphone apps for enhanced public safety planning.

Article Title: His father could no longer go where wheelchairs stopped. So he turned an industrial robot into a walking mobility chair
Article Text:
Image: Jake Laser

An industrial robot becomes an all-terrain mobility aid

Laser built it with his father in mind

Poll Vote & Share your view Would you support further investment in robotics to improve mobility for people with disabilities? Yes No 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

How the 'bionic leg chair' works

A glimpse into the future of accessible mobility

For most wheelchair users, everyday life is designed around the limits of accessible infrastructure. A dropped kerb or a ramp may solve one obstacle, but steep hills, rocky trails, forest paths and staircases can still put entire destinations out of reach. Rather than accept those limitations, American maker and engineer Jake Laser set out to build something entirely different for his father, who has lived with multiple sclerosis (MS) for the past 20 years.The result is what Laser calls the "bionic leg chair," a robotic mobility vehicle that combines advanced quadruped technology with a custom-built seat, allowing his father to travel across terrain that conventional wheelchairs cannot navigate. By adapting an industrial robot rather than designing a mobility aid from scratch, Laser has demonstrated how robotics originally developed for industry could one day help redefine personal accessibility.At the heart of the project is an industrial-grade quadruped robot built by Unitree, a robotics company known for developing agile, four-legged machines. Unlike traditional robots that rely solely on legs, this platform uses a hybrid leg-and-wheel design, with powered wheels mounted beneath each robotic foot.That combination gives it the best of both worlds. On pavements and smooth indoor floors, the robot rolls efficiently like a wheeled vehicle, reducing energy consumption and providing a comfortable ride. When it encounters stairs, rocks, tree roots or uneven ground, it can transition to walking mode, lifting each leg independently to climb obstacles that would stop even specialised off-road wheelchairs.For Laser, the platform offered an ideal foundation for a mobility device capable of travelling almost anywhere a person on foot could reasonably go.Laser's motivation was deeply personal. His father has lived with multiple sclerosis for two decades, a neurological condition that can progressively affect movement, balance and muscle control. Although wheelchairs restore mobility in many situations, they remain constrained by the terrain around them.Wanting to give his father greater independence outdoors, Laser transformed the quadruped robot into a personal transport system by installing a custom seat, developing modified control software and designing an automotive-inspired body around the machine. The result is a vehicle that allows its occupant to remain seated while the robot walks, rolls and climbs beneath them.More than an engineering exercise, the project was designed to restore access to places that had gradually become inaccessible, from hiking trails and countryside paths to uneven natural landscapes where traditional mobility aids struggle.The robotic chair retains the quadruped's original ability to balance dynamically while moving. The on-board computer constantly adjusts the legs in order to keep the robot stable, regardless of whether it travels over difficult terrain or climbs stairs.Laser redesigned the control system in order to simplify the robot’s use as an assistive device, and the special seating system ensures safe positioning of the passenger. However, despite the futuristic style of the design, which makes the robot look like a car instead of heavy machinery, it is still an advanced robotic device made to perform in harsh conditions.The wheel/legs design allows the robot to move smoothly on smooth ground while keeping the capability to overcome obstacles that would require leaving the wheelchair behind.Laser documented the project on Youtube channel on his channel @JLaservideo, where it has drawn widespread interest from engineers, robotics enthusiasts and accessibility advocates alike. Although the bionic leg chair is a bespoke prototype rather than a commercial product, it highlights the growing potential for robotics to transform mobility beyond conventional wheelchairs.There is a growing trend of researchers developing legged robots for use cases such as disaster relief and recovery, industrial inspection, and even healthcare and rehabilitation. These efforts imply that one day, similar technology will offer individuals with disabilities greater opportunities to explore inaccessible locations.The case of Jake Laser is unique not only because the innovation represents a technological breakthrough but also because it was motivated by a need to help someone in the developer's family. The young man's desire to give his father a chance to visit certain places once again led to a brilliant idea of turning an industrial quadruped into a walking mobility chair.
Article Summary: Jake Laser's father has lived with multiple sclerosis for two decades. He wanted to give his father greater independence outdoors. Laser turned an industrial quadruped robot into a personal transport system. The result is a vehicle that allows its occupant to remain seated while the robot walks, rolls and climbs.

Article Title: Rishabh Agarwal opens up about rejecting $1 million proposal from Meta: The offer was higher....
Article Text:
Rishabh Agarwal addressed the viral claim that he left $1M job offer from Meta to start his own Periodic Labs (right) which is building an AI scientist.

Indian techie Rishabh Agarwal is a known name in the AI space, as the IIT Bombay graduate has been building Periodic Labs, an AI startup. Last year, around August, Agarwal announced his "tough decision" to exit Meta and take a different kind of risk by making something of his own. As Agarwal's impressive CV started going viral again, Agarwal addressed the claim that he left a $1 million offer from Meta and clarified that the offer "was an order of magnitude higher than $1 million", suggesting that he let more than just $1 million go to build his own company."Meet Rishabh Agrawal, who got AIR 33 in JEE and completed his B. Tech, Computer Science from IIT Bombay. He worked at Google Brain, DeepMind, Waymo, and when Mark Zuckerberg personally gave him a $1 million job offer, he denied it. And do you know what he's doing today? Building the next-generation AI company called Periodic Labs," the viral post said."And do you know what he's doing today? Building the next-generation AI company called Periodic Labs.""Many of us might not know him well, but in the AI world, he is a big deal. The reason is that he has worked at some of the most elite AI labs on the planet: Google Brain, DeepMind, Waymo, and Meta Superintelligence Labs."In spite of that, when he was offered a ₹9.5 crore per annum job, after five months at Meta, he denied it, quit, and joined Periodic Labs as a co-founder," the post said, adding that Agarwal chose to be a big fish in a small pond rather than being a small fish in a big pond.Backed by Jeff Bezos and NVIDIA, Agarwal's Periodic Labs is building the future of next-generation AI as it is building an AI scientist that can generate hypotheses for real-world experiments to discover new materials, medicines, and even new physics.
Article Summary: Rishabh Agarwal addressed the viral claim that he left $1M job offer from Meta to start his own Periodic Labs which is building an AI scientist. He clarified that the offer "was an order of magnitude higher than $1 million", suggesting that he let more than just $1m go.

Article Title: Nominee vs legal heir: What is the difference and who has claim on assets? All you need to know
Article Text:
AI image

Who is a Nominee?



Who is a Legal Heir?

Difference between a Nominee and a Legal heir

Aspect Nominee Legal Heir Role Custodian Owner Transfer of Assets Can be done without any legal formalities. Requires testamentary documents for proof of ownership. Powers No power to transact, use, or transfer assets. Power to use and transact assets as an owner. Appointment Owner can appoint anyone, but some institutions specify certain conditions. Determined by succession/personal laws. Legal Right Cannot contest ownership legally. Can contest ownership in a court of law.

When individuals open bank accounts, invest in mutual funds, or buy insurance, they are often asked to name a nominee.However, many people mistakenly assume that the nominee automatically inherits the assets. In reality, the nominee is not always the final recipient — the ownership rights belong to the legal heir, reported ET.Understanding the distinction between these two is essential to ensure that your wealth is passed on to the intended beneficiaries.A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets.The nominee’s role is limited to holding and managing the assets until they are legally transferred to the rightful heirs.According to ET, the nominee cannot sell, trade, or make any transactional decisions about the assets.The account or policy owner can appoint anyone — family, friends, or acquaintances — as a nominee, though financial institutions often prefer close family members.In some cases, the nominee and the legal heir can be the same person, which helps in ensuring a smoother transfer of assets.The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away.To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will or other testamentary proof.If there is no will — a situation known as dying intestate — the heir is determined as per the country’s succession or personal laws, reported ET.While a nominee can initially access the assets, only the legal heir can claim them permanently or challenge ownership in court.Experts quoted by ET emphasise that having clarity on this difference helps avoid disputes and ensures that the savings reach the intended family members or beneficiaries.Therefore, it is advisable to regularly update both nominees and wills to align with one’s estate planning goals.
Article Summary: A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets. The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away. To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will.

Article Title: Life Certificate 2025: Step-by-step guide to generate and submit Jeevan Pramaan online and offline
Article Text:
What is a Life Certificate and why does it matter



Annual submission deadline for pensioners in 2025



1 November and 30 November.

How to generate Jeevan Pramaan online: Step-by-step process



Step 1: Biometric device registration

Step 2: Authentication and certificate generation

Electronic delivery and access of Digital Life Certificate



How can you submit a Life Certificate in 2025



Offline submission at a bank branch or CSC centre

Online submission via Jeevan Pramaan Portal

Submission through the UMANG App

Who needs to submit the Life Certificate



Central and State Government pensioners

Defence pensioners

Family pensioners in the event of the pensioner’s death

Pensioners from public sector undertakings

Key details required before submitting the Life Certificate



Aadhaar Number

Registered Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar

Pension Payment Order (PPO) Number

Bank Account Number where the pension is credited

Name of the Pension Sanctioning Authority

Is Aadhaar mandatory for a digital Life Certificate



Legal validity of Jeevan Pramaan



How Jeevan Pramaan simplifies pensioner verification



Every year, lakhs of pensioners across India must prove that they are still alive to continue receiving their pension. This verification, known as the Life Certificate or Digital Life Certificate (DLC), is a crucial and mandatory step in ensuring that pension payments are made only to eligible recipients, preventing fraud and misuse.Traditionally, this process involved visiting the bank or pension office in person, standing in long queues, and submitting a signed paper certificate. However, with the government’s focus on digitisation and technological advancement, this process has become much simpler, faster, and more convenient. Pensioners can now submit their certificates digitally from the comfort of their homes using Jeevan Pramaan, a secure, user-friendly, and Aadhaar-based platform.A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries.The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric-enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.Introduced by the Government of India, it uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication using either fingerprint or iris scan to verify a pensioner’s identity. Once verified, a digital certificate is automatically generated and shared with authorised pension disbursing agencies.This online process eliminates the need for physical verification, providing elderly pensioners with a simpler and more accessible way to maintain their pension benefits.According to PIB, The submission of the Life Certificate is mandatory for all pensioners who wish to continue receiving their pension without interruption. For the year 2025, the standard submission period is betweenTo make the process more comfortable for the elderly, the government allows pensioners aged 80 years and above to begin the submission process earlier, from 1 October 2025. This extended window gives senior citizens more time and flexibility to complete the procedure without facing last-minute difficulties.The Jeevan Pramaan process involves two main steps: registering the biometric device and generating the digital certificate.Before using the service, pensioners or operators must register their biometric device such as a fingerprint or iris scanner. During registration, the user provides their Aadhaar number and mobile number, after which an OTP (One-Time Password) is sent to their mobile. After entering the OTP, they must provide their name and email ID before scanning their fingerprint or iris.Once authentication is complete, the system confirms that device registration was successful, allowing the user to move to the next step.In this step, the pensioner enters personal details such as Aadhaar number, mobile number, name, PPO number, pension type, sanctioning authority, disbursing agency, and bank account details. The system also asks whether the pensioner has remarried or is re-employed.After entering all details, the pensioner scans their fingerprint or iris using the registered biometric device. Upon successful authentication, the Digital Life Certificate is generated, and an SMS acknowledgment with the Jeevan Pramaan Certificate ID is sent to the registered mobile number.This certificate is automatically stored in the Life Certificate Repository, accessible anytime to both pensioners and pension agencies.Once the Digital Life Certificate is created, it can be electronically delivered to the concerned Pension Disbursing Agency (PDA). PDAs can log in to the Jeevan Pramaan portal to view, verify, or download the certificate directly.Pensioners can also download a PDF copy of their certificate by visiting the Jeevan Pramaan website and entering their Pramaan ID or Aadhaar number. This ensures transparency and easy access for future reference.The government provides several options to submit the Life Certificate, allowing pensioners to choose the method that suits them best.Pensioners who prefer traditional methods can visit their bank branch or a Citizen Service Centre (CSC) to submit their Life Certificate physically. Trained staff at these centres assist pensioners in verifying their details and completing the process.For those unable to travel due to age or health issues, India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) offers a doorstep banking service. Pensioners can schedule an appointment, and an IPPB representative will visit their home to complete the submission using biometric authentication. This service is available for both IPPB and non-IPPB account holders.For pensioners comfortable with technology, the Jeevan Pramaan portal provides a completely online process. By visiting https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in, pensioners can submit their Digital Life Certificate using a computer, laptop, or smartphone equipped with a fingerprint or iris scanner.The system uses the Aadhaar platform to authenticate biometric details. Once verification is successful, a Digital Life Certificate is generated and stored in a secure online repository. Pension disbursing agencies can then directly access the certificate, removing the need for physical paperwork.The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app offers another easy option. Pensioners can download the app from the Google Play Store, search for “Jeevan Pramaan,” and select “Generate Life Certificate.”After entering the necessary details such as Aadhaar number, PPO number, and type of pension, pensioners must authenticate themselves using biometric verification. The app then generates a Digital Life Certificate (DLC), which is automatically shared with the pension disbursing authority.All pensioners who receive government, defence, or private sector pensions through authorised disbursing agencies are required to submit their Life Certificate every year. This includes:Failure to submit the certificate within the stipulated period may result in temporary suspension of pension payments until verification is completed.To ensure a smooth submission process, pensioners should keep certain details and documents ready. These include:Having these details ready helps to avoid delays or errors during authentication, whether submitting online or offline.Yes, an Aadhaar number or Virtual ID (VID) is mandatory to generate a Digital Life Certificate. The entire Jeevan Pramaan system is based on Aadhaar authentication, which ensures accuracy and prevents fraud. Pensioners must make sure their Aadhaar is linked with their mobile number and bank account before attempting to generate the certificate.The Digital Life Certificate is fully valid and legally recognised under the Information Technology Act. Pension disbursing authorities across India accept it as an official document, equivalent to the traditional paper-based life certificate.Its biometric verification system makes it secure and tamper-proof, reducing the chances of wrongful pension disbursal or misuse of pension funds.Before the introduction of Jeevan Pramaan, pensioners often faced difficulties in submitting their certificates, especially those with health problems or limited mobility. The digital system has removed these challenges, allowing pensioners to submit their verification anytime and anywhere.By integrating biometric technology and Aadhaar authentication, Jeevan Pramaan has streamlined pension management, improving efficiency for both pensioners and government departments. It also enhances transparency and ensures that genuine beneficiaries receive payments on time.
Article Summary: A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries. The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric- enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.

Article Text:
If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. (AI image)

2. Register with the platform

ALSO READ |

Points to keep in mind

Update your will regularly: You can keep your will up-to-date by regularly updating it on the platform to reflect any changes in your life or financial situation.

Communicate with family: It's also important to store your will safely and let your family know where to find it when needed.

Creating a will online: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. Here's how to make an online will.Begin by choosing a reliable online service. Look for a platform that has a simple interface, comprehensive templates, and secure storage for your documents.To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.Next, make a list of all your assets. Include property, investments, bank accounts, insurance policies, and any valuable items you own.Decide who will inherit your assets by choosing your beneficiaries. You can assign specific items to each beneficiary and select backup beneficiaries in case your first choices can't inherit. Then, choose an executor, the person who will carry out your will.Use one of the customizable templates available on the platform. Follow the instructions to fill in the details, specifying how you want your assets distributed and including any special conditions or terms.You can review the drafted will to ensure it's accurate and make changes if needed. If you have complicated issues, it's best to get legal advice. Once you're satisfied, you can legally execute the will according to the platform's rules, usually by electronically signing it with witnesses. The completed will is then stored securely online, with encryption for added security.With inputs from Centre for Investment Education and Learning content which appeared in Economic Times
Article Summary: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.

Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.
